title: narcissus asturiensis
intro: Narcissus asturiensis, also known as Asturian daffodil or Western daffodil, is a perennial plant belonging to the Amaryllidaceae family. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its alkaloids, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antitumor activities. The plant is said to offer various health benefits, including reducing pain, improving cognitive function, and possessing antioxidant properties. In traditional herbal preparations, the bulbs and flowers of Narcissus asturiensis are used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, often employed in the treatment of respiratory issues, fever, and skin conditions.
